fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: District of Columbia Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to District of Columbia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity district of columbia, how to transfer dietitian license to district of columbia, dietitian compact district of columbia, district of columbia dietitian endorsement application

board.name: Board of Dietetics & Nutrition
board.parent_dept: DC Department of Health, Health Regulation and Licensing Administration (HRLA)
board.mailing_address: DC Department of Health, Health Regulation and Licensing Administration, 899 North Capitol Street NE, 3rd Floor, Washington, DC 20002

quick_facts.ce_requirement: 30 Continuing Education Units (CEUs) every 2 years, including 2 CEUs in LGBTQ Cultural Competency.

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian
license_types.description: Requires current CDR registration (RD/RDN credential), including passage of the CDR examination and completion of an ACEND-accredited program and supervised practice. As of January 1, 2024, CDR requires a minimum master's degree for all new registrants. Fee: $229.

license_types.title: Licensed Nutritionist
license_types.description: Requires a bachelor's degree minimum in human nutrition, foods and nutrition, dietetics, or food systems management from an accredited institution, plus 900 hours of supervised practice. Must pass the CDR Registration Examination. DC also accepts CNS certification. Fee: $264 (includes $26 exam fee).

steps.title: Meet Eligibility Requirements
steps.description: Ensure you meet the academic (bachelor's or master's degree in dietetics/nutrition), supervised practice (1200 hours), and examination (pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) exam) requirements as outlined by the Board of Dietetics and Nutrition.

steps.title: Create Account and Start Online Application
steps.description: Visit the HRLA Online Services Portal (hrla.doh.dc.gov/hrla-portal/s/) and sign up for an account. Once logged in, select 'Apply for a New License' and choose 'Dietitian/Nutritionist' to begin your application.

steps.title: Complete Application Form and Upload Documents
steps.description: Fill out the comprehensive application form within the HRLA Online Services Portal. Upload all required supporting documents, including official transcripts, verification of supervised practice, CDR exam results, and a government-issued ID.

steps.title: Submit Background Check and Pay Fees
steps.description: Complete the required criminal background check as instructed by the HRLA. Pay the non-refundable $85 application fee and the $155 initial licensure fee (due upon approval) through the HRLA Online Services Portal.

steps.title: Board Review and License Issuance
steps.description: Your complete application and supporting documents will be reviewed by the Board of Dietetics and Nutrition. Upon approval, your license will be issued, allowing you to practice as a licensed dietitian/nutritionist in DC.

documents.detail: Original photos with applicant's name on back.
Available in the DC Health initial license application package.
Available in the DC Health initial license application package.
Supervisor verifies at least satisfactory performance.
From each jurisdiction where you hold or held a license — required for endorsement applicants.
FBI fingerprinting via FieldPrint, initiated through the online portal.

provisional_text: The District of Columbia does not issue temporary practice permits for dietitians. You cannot practice or use protected titles until your license has been formally issued by the Board.
reciprocity_text: DC issues dietitian licenses by endorsement for those licensed in other jurisdictions at the same $229 fee. Applicants must still meet all DC requirements, submit a full application, provide letter(s) of good standing from each jurisdiction, and maintain current CDR registration.

faqs.question: How much does a DC dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial fee is $229, consisting of an $85 application fee and a $144 license fee. The license fee is refundable if denied or withdrawn. A separate criminal background check fee is paid through the portal. Nutritionist applicants pay $264 (includes $26 exam fee). Duplicate licenses cost $34 each.

faqs.question: Is a criminal background check required for a DC dietitian license?
faqs.answer: Yes. Per D.C. Code § 3-1205.22 and 17 DCMR 85, a criminal background check with FBI fingerprinting is required for all initial license applicants. U.S.-based applicants schedule through FieldPrint via the online portal. International applicants use FBI FD-258 fingerprint cards.

faqs.question: What are the continuing education requirements for DC dietitians?
faqs.answer: DC requires 30 CEUs per biennial renewal cycle, including a mandatory 2 CEUs in LGBTQ Cultural Competency. Licenses expire November 1 of odd-numbered years. First-time renewals are exempt from CE requirements. CDR registration requires 75 CPEUs every five years separately.

faqs.question: Does District of Columbia have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in District of Columbia. To work in District of Columbia, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to District of Columbia?
Can I start working in District of Columbia while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

reciprocity.notes: DC issues dietitian licenses by endorsement for those licensed in other jurisdictions at the same $229 fee. Applicants must still meet all DC requirements, submit a full application, provide letter(s) of good standing from each jurisdiction, and maintain current CDR registration.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in District of Columbia
hero.dek: Dietitians and Nutritionists seeking licensure in the District of Columbia must apply online through the HRLA Online Services Portal, managed by the DC Department of Health's Health Regulation and Licensing Administration.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the HRLA Online Services Portal and click 'Sign Up' in the upper right corner. Enter your name, email address, and create a password. Verify your account via the link sent to your email. Once verified, log in to the portal.
